title: Minimize the admission of containers with capabilities assigned

input_row.title: Minimize the admission of containers with capabilities assigned
input_row.description: Do not generally permit containers with capabilities
input_row.rationale: Containers run with a default set of capabilities as assigned by the Container Runtime. Capabilities are parts of the rights generally granted on a Linux system to the root user. In many cases applications running in containers do not require any capabilities to operate, so from the perspective of the principal of least privilege use of capabilities should be minimized.
input_row.audit: Get the set of SCCs with the following command: oc get scc For each SCC, check whether capabilities have been forbidden: oc describe scc <name> | grep “Required Drop Capabilities” for i in `oc get scc --template '{{range .items}}{{.metadata.name}}{{"\n"}}{{end}}'`; do echo "$i"; oc describe scc $i | grep "Required Drop Capabilities"; done
input_row.remediation: Review the use of capabilities in applications running on your cluster. Where a namespace contains applications which do not require any Linux capabilities to operate consider adding a SCC which forbids the admission of containers which do not drop all capabilities.
input_row.impact: Pods with containers which require capabilities to operate will not be permitted.
input_row.default_value: 273 | P a g e By default, OpenShift 4 clusters include the following SCCs: anyuid Required Drop Capabilities: MKNOD hostaccess Required Drop Capabilities: KILL,MKNOD,SETUID,SETGID hostmount-anyuid Required Drop Capabilities: MKNOD hostnetwork Required Drop Capabilities: KILL,MKNOD,SETUID,SETGID node-exporter Required Drop Capabilities: <none> non-root Required Drop Capabilities: KILL,MKNOD,SETUID,SETGID privileged Required Drop Capabilities: <none> restricted Required Drop Capabilities: KILL,MKNOD,SETUID,SETGID

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Step to access Security Context Constraints (SCC):**
   - Use the command: `oc get scc` to list the existing SCCs in your OpenShift environment.

2. **Step to verify Required Drop Capabilities for each SCC:**
   - For each SCC listed, run the command: `oc describe scc <name> | grep "Required Drop Capabilities"`, replacing `<name>` with the name of the SCC from the previous step. Note down the output.

3. **Step to validate capability assignments:**
   - Manually review the output for each SCC to check if required capabilities have been specified as `none` or appropriately restricted.

4. **Expected secure configuration:**
   - Ensure that SCCs in namespaces containing applications that do not require any capabilities have `Required Drop Capabilities` set to drop all capabilities (e.g., non-root SCC).

5. **How to confirm compliance:**
   - If any SCC allows capabilities to be assigned without restrictions, document the SCC name and the specifics of the capabilities. If the SCCs follow the guidance by dropping all capabilities and restricting as necessary, then compliance can be confirmed.
